question_text: A ball is thrown upwards into the air. At the moment it leaves your hand, it has kinetic energy. As it rises, what happens to its kinetic energy and gravitational potential energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Kinetic energy increases, potential energy decreases

options.label: B
options.text: Kinetic energy decreases, potential energy increases

options.label: C
options.text: Both kinetic energy and potential energy increase

options.label: D
options.text: Both kinetic energy and potential energy decrease

explanation: As the ball rises, it slows down, so its kinetic energy decreases. At the same time, it gets higher, so its gravitational potential energy increases. Energy is conserved by transforming from one form to the other.
